summary: OpenSea SDK impersonator harvests wallets, SSH/AWS credentials, and seed phrases at install
details: The package impersonates the OpenSea SDK namespace but ships no SDK code — the sole module is postinstall.js, wired to both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks. On install, the script walks the user's home directory, Desktop, Documents, Downloads, /root, and /tmp for wallet, keystore, seed, mnemonic, and.env files, reads Solana id.json and Ethereum keystores, ~/.ssh/id_* private keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and MetaMask/Phantom browser extension storage, and POSTs the file contents to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777. It also scans.txt/.md/.rtf/.csv files for BIP-39 wordlist matches and wallet-related keywords (in English and Spanish) and uploads matching content to the same endpoint. Host identifiers (os.hostname, os.userInfo) are collected alongside the exfiltrated data. The package name @wagni_bot/opensea-sdk is a namespace impersonation of the OpenSea SDK used as the delivery vehicle.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
